flashcards.q: What is the atomic number of carbon and how many valence electrons does it have?
flashcards.a: The atomic number is 6 and it has 4 valence electrons.

flashcards.q: Why does carbon form covalent bonds instead of ionic bonds?
flashcards.a: Because losing or gaining 4 electrons is energetically unfavourable and difficult for the nucleus to handle.

flashcards.q: Name the three common allotropes of carbon.

flashcards.a: The ability of carbon to form bonds with other carbon atoms, creating long chains or rings.

flashcards.q: What is the difference between saturated and unsaturated hydrocarbons?
flashcards.a: Saturated hydrocarbons have only single bonds, while unsaturated ones contain one or more double or triple bonds.

flashcards.q: State the general formula for alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes.
flashcards.a: Alkanes: CₙH₂ₙ₊₂; Alkenes: CₙH₂ₙ; Alkynes: CₙH₂ₙ₋₂.
Compounds with the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements.

flashcards.q: Name the functional group present in alcohols.

flashcards.a: A group of compounds where the same functional group replaces hydrogen in a carbon chain, differing by a -CH₂- unit.

flashcards.q: Identify the oxidising agents used to convert ethanol to ethanoic acid.
flashcards.a: Alkaline potassium permanganate or acidified potassium dichromate.

flashcards.q: What is hydrogenation and where is it industrially applied?
flashcards.a: The addition of hydrogen to unsaturated hydrocarbons using a catalyst, used to convert vegetable oils into solid fats.
The reaction between a carboxylic acid and an alcohol to produce a sweet-smelling ester.

flashcards.q: How does soap clean oily dirt?
flashcards.a: By forming micelles where the hydrophobic tails entrap the oil and the hydrophilic heads face water.

flashcards.q: Why don't soaps work effectively in hard water?
flashcards.a: They react with calcium and magnesium ions to form an insoluble precipitate called scum.

flashcards.q: What component of detergents allows them to work in hard water?
flashcards.a: Their charged ends do not form insoluble solids with calcium and magnesium ions.
